failure_modes.text: Maslow's hammer can turn simplification into forcing the nearest easy frame onto the wrong problem.
failure_modes.source_quote: Mistaking the Tool for the Problem
failure_modes.mitigation: Test frame fit before simplifying: verify that the simplified model shares the structural features of the actual problem, not just surface similarity.

failure_modes.text: Premature abstraction can make the message elegant while stripping away the concrete guidance operators actually need.
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve operational specificity: simplify the explanation, but keep the concrete guidance, boundary conditions, and caveats that operators need to act safely.

failure_modes.text: Oversimplifying risk can remove the caveat or boundary that makes the recommendation safe to use.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when simplification removes the very caveat, boundary, or uncertainty that makes the decision safe to use in reality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Flag removed caveats explicitly: when simplifying, note what was stripped away and under what conditions the simplified version becomes dangerous or misleading.

premortem_questions.text: If the audience remembers only one thing, what should it be?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If the audience remembers only one thing, what should it be?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the Worst Case Bad Enough?

heuristics.text: Find the core before carrying the full edifice of analysis into the handoff.
Use the rule of three to give the audience a durable verbal roadmap rather than a bag of details.
Use the Feynman technique to expose blind spots before compressing the explanation for someone else.

curation_notes.summary: The raw file is strong on core-finding and anti-clutter intervention content, so the chunk set stays close to those explicit simplification disciplines.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor communication-style examples that repeated the same simplification mechanism in more topical language.
open_questions: This model remains normalization-heavy on failure modes, but the raw mitigation and danger lines support the current chunk set directly enough for Wave 2.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on cognitive load theory, systems thinking, and synthesis as future relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strong on boiling-the-ocean pressure and MECE-related tension for later relation work.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against complex-adaptive-systems and divergent-vs-convergent-thinking.
